evals.prompt: Read the fixture in evals/files/single-file-on-main/input.json and apply the commit skill as a dry run. Do not run git or mutate a repo. Save outputs/result.json and outputs/output.md. result.json must include: status, stop_reason, next_action, branch, branch_action, selected_paths, commit_type, subject, commit_message, push_requested, should_push, pr_requested, should_create_pr, pr_blocker, pr_title, pr_body. Use status=commit or status=stop. Use branch_action=create or branch_action=keep. push_requested means the user explicitly asked to push; should_push means push is actually required.
evals.expected_output: Plans one feature commit from an unstaged single-file change on main, creates a feature branch, pushes because the user asked, and does not create a PR.
assertions: Returns a commit decision instead of a stop.
Creates a non-main feature branch using issue 123.
Uses commit type feat plus Summary, Rationale, Tests, Refs #123, and the default Copilot trailer.
Plans a push but no PR.

evals.prompt: Read the fixture in evals/files/staged-bugfix-pr/input.json and apply the commit skill as a dry run. Do not run git or mutate a repo. Save outputs/result.json and outputs/output.md. result.json must include: status, stop_reason, next_action, branch, branch_action, selected_paths, commit_type, subject, commit_message, push_requested, should_push, pr_requested, should_create_pr, pr_blocker, pr_title, pr_body. Use status=commit or status=stop. Use branch_action=create or branch_action=keep. push_requested means the user explicitly asked to push; should_push means push is actually required. pr_title must equal subject, not the full conventional-commit first line.
evals.expected_output: Keeps the current bugfix branch, commits only the staged files, uses a fix commit with Fixes #456, and prepares a PR.
assertions: Returns a commit decision instead of a stop.
Selects only the staged bugfix files and excludes unstaged README noise.
Uses commit type fix plus Summary, Rationale, Tests, Fixes #456, and the default Copilot trailer.
Plans both push and PR creation, with PR title matching the commit subject.

evals.prompt: Read the fixture in evals/files/ambiguous-multi-surface/input.json and apply the commit skill as a dry run. Do not run git or mutate a repo. Save outputs/result.json and outputs/output.md. result.json must include: status, stop_reason, next_action, branch, branch_action, selected_paths, commit_type, subject, commit_message, push_requested, should_push, pr_requested, should_create_pr, pr_blocker, pr_title, pr_body. Use status=commit or status=stop. Use branch_action=create or branch_action=keep. push_requested means the user explicitly asked to push; should_push means push is actually required.
evals.expected_output: Stops because the unstaged changes span multiple top-level directories and the user did not choose a scope.
assertions: Explains that the file scope is ambiguous.
Asks the user which files to commit next.
Does not plan a push or PR.

evals.prompt: Read the fixture in evals/files/generated-artifact-stop/input.json and apply the commit skill as a dry run. Do not run git or mutate a repo. Save outputs/result.json and outputs/output.md. result.json must include: status, stop_reason, next_action, branch, branch_action, selected_paths, commit_type, subject, commit_message, push_requested, should_push, pr_requested, should_create_pr, pr_blocker, pr_title, pr_body. Use status=commit or status=stop. Use branch_action=create or branch_action=keep. push_requested means the user explicitly asked to push; should_push means push is actually required.
evals.expected_output: Stops because the staged set includes generated artifacts that require confirmation before commit.
assertions: Flags the staged generated artifacts instead of committing them automatically.
Asks for confirmation before versioning those artifacts.
Does not plan a push or PR.
